wiki.content: "Stupid Song" (stylized in all lowercase) is a song by American singer-songwriter Olivia Rodrigo and the third single from her third studio album, "You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love". Rodrigo wrote the song with its producer, Dan Nigro. It was released alongside the album by Geffen Records on June 12, 2026, and was accompanied by a music video. "Stupid Song" reached number one in Australia, Ireland, New Zealand, and on the Global 200, and entered the top ten in Austria, Canada, Germany, the Netherlands, Portugal, Singapore, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, and the United States. In March 2026, Rodrigo discussed the thematic direction of "You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love" in an interview with British Vogue. She described the album as primarily consisting of "sad love songs", noting that many of her favourite romantic songs are characterised by feelings of fear or longing. On April 2, 2026, the album title was revealed through promotional activities in Los Angeles and other cities. Rodrigo continued her collaboration with Dan Nigro, who served as the album's producer. She released "Drop Dead" as the lead single on April 17, followed by "The Cure" as the second single on May 22. The track listing for "You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love" was revealed on May 26, with "Stupid Song" appearing as the second track in the "Girl So in Love" section of the album. Rodrigo stated that she was inspired to write the song after reading Annie Ernaux's 1992 novel "Simple Passion", which explores an affair marked by longing and obsession. She aimed to portray the emotional intensity and negative feelings that can occur within romantic relationships. "Stupid Song" was released as the album's third single on June 12, 2026. "Stupid Song" has a duration of three minutes and twenty-nine seconds. Nigro produced the track, engineered it with Chris Kasych, and programmed it with Bryn Bliska. Nigro also performed bass and guitar, contributed drum programming and the string arrangement, while Sterling Laws played drums, Noah Conrad played piano, and Paul Cartwright performed on viola and violin. Mitch McCarthy mixed the song, and Mike Bozzi mastered it with assistance from Jack Doutt. Musically, "Stupid Song" has been described as an indie pop, pop rock, and synth-rock song. Its lyrics focus on Rodrigo's infatuation with a romantic partner. The song describes her feelings of insecurity at a party, her thoughts about conversations with her partner, and her growing emotional attachment. Rodrigo uses imagery involving fire and speed to represent the intensity and uncontrollability of her feelings. "Stupid Song" received positive reviews from critics. Hannah Dailey of Billboard ranked it as the best song on "You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl in Love", describing it as a track that contrasted with its title. She praised the arrangement, pacing, and Rodrigo's vocal performance. Alexis Petridis of The Guardian described the song as having a show tune-like melody and cited it as an example of the album's songwriting quality. "Stupid Song" debuted at number three on the US Billboard Hot 100 chart dated June 27, 2026. Its entry contributed to Rodrigo increasing her number of career top-ten entries to ten, alongside "Honeybee" and the album's previous singles "Drop Dead" and "The Cure". The song also debuted at number two on the Canadian Hot 100 and the UK Singles Chart. Internationally, "Stupid Song" debuted at number one in Australia, New Zealand, and on the Billboard Global 200. It also reached number one in Ireland, number two in Greece, Hong Kong, Portugal, and Singapore, number three in Austria, number four in Switzerland, number six in Germany, number seven in the Netherlands, and number eight in Luxembourg and Malaysia.
